calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2014 Vacated

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Nov-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-14-297520 The Estate of Ellen A. Hood. and PTR-14-297521 The Ellen A. Hood Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Special Administrator/Temporary Trustee). Kevin Rodriguez for SFSPCA. George Hood in Propria Persona.

Mr. Rodriguez informed the Court that a notice of errata re petition to approve settlement showing Mr. Hood's signature was filed on September 16, 2014. The Parties discussed the issue of the Trust being under Court supervision and bond. The Court stated that it will modify the order regarding findings. Mr. Der stated that there are no assets in the Estate and that everything was in the Trust. Mr. Hood requested that the Court dismiss the petition to remove trustee. The Court granted the Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement (subject to review and modification of the order). Status on discovery is off calendar. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to January 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of a petition to terminate estate for lack of assets. Mr. Hoods request is granted. The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. is off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Discovery
Off Calendar Nov-18-2014 Off Calendar

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (3790-3792 - 21st Street, Sf, Ca)
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ernest Der for and with Debra Dolch. Goerge Hood also present.

The sale was confirmed at $1,300,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $435,000.00 is required.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

The correct amount of the required additional bond is $635,000.00.

Petition To Remove Trustee, For Damages, Equitable Relief To Invalidate Trust & Appoint Fiduciary
Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Continued To Jan-12-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-14-297520 The Estate of Ellen A. Hood and PTR-14-297521 The Ellen A. Hood Revocable Trust.

Appearances: George Hood in Propria Persona. Joseph Corey for Edward Corey (Trustee). Kevin Rodriguez for SFSPCA, Humane Society of the United States, Fund for Animals, PETA & ASPCA. William Hess appeared by telephone for Dedication & Everlasting Love to Animals. Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Special Administrator/Guardian Ad Litem).

Mr. Hood stated that he was unable to obtain representation. Mr. Rodriguez stated that he would like a trial date on the various petitions and get an order requiring Mr. Hood to provide written responses to discovery and appear at the deposition. Mr. Hood explained financial circumstances and asked for more time. Counsel presented further argument. The Motions to Compel Depositions and Production of Documents, etc. in both matters were taken off calendar without prejudice to Mr. Hood re-noticing them. The Petition to Remove Trustee, for Damages, etc. was set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on January 12, 2015 (subject to written order). Written discovery is due by September 22, 2014. Deposition is reserved for October 13, 2014. The Parties will work on resolving the matter. Status on discovery was set for October 28, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is also continued to October 28, 2014.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Aug-12-2014 Off Calendar

Petition To Remove Trustee, For Damages, Equitable Relief To Invalidate Trust & Appoint Fiduciary
Off Calendar Jun-09-2014 Continued To Aug-12-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Depositions And Production Of Documents; George Hood'S Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Depositions And Production Of Documents And Request For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jul-09-2014 Continued To Aug-12-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The two following cases were called together: PES-14-297520 Estate of Ellen A Hood and PTR-14-297521 The Ellen A. Hood Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Maria Bellafronto appeared by Court Call for 6 animal charities. George Hood in Pro Per (Moving Party). Joseph Corey for Edward Corey. Jessica Farinas (Mr. Corey's Legal Assistant).

Mr. Hood updated the Court with his effort to seek new counsel, that Mr. O'Sullivan has declined to represent him on June 29, that it is no possible to get anyone to step in, that he will continue to look for new attorney and that he is not prepared to go forward. The Court questioned where discovery stands. Mr. Corey stated that he has served discovery on Mr. Shjeflo (Mr. Hood's former counsel) and received a blanket objection. Ms. Bellafronto stated that she also served discovery and that she has received a request for continuance from Mr. O"Sullivan for production of the file. Mr. Corey stated that he filed opposition to the motion on June 25, 2014, that they agreed to appear and that they only need to schedule the date for depositions. The Court indicated that the July 23, 2014 hearing on the underlined petitions may need to be continued. Mr. Carey stated that discovery needs to move forward. Mr. Bellafronto stated that she can not get a date from Mr. Hood. The Court continued the July 23, 2014 hearing to August 12, 2014 at 10:30 a.m. and today's two discovery motions are also continued to the same date. The Court gave Mr. Hood one last change to obtain counsel and that the case will go forward without counsel on August 12, 2014. The parties are to come in to pick a date for deposition. Ms. Bellafronto argued that she has not received any record and that she wants a trial date. The Court encouraged parties to reconsider mediation. Ms. Bellafronto stated that she needs to confer with her clients and she raised the issue with the value of the property is reduced due to Mr. Hoods' occupancy in the property. The Court stated that Ms. Dolch will need to go forward with the sale so the property will not be lost to foreclosure.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition To Remove Trustee, For Damages, Equitable Relief To Invalidate Trust & Appoint Fiduciary
Off Calendar May-27-2014 Continued To Jul-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-14-297520 The Estate of Ellen A. Hood and PTR-14-297521 The Ellen A. Hood Revocable Trust

Appearances: Walter Shjeflo for and with George Hood. Joseph Corey for Edward Corey (Trustee). William Hess appeared by telephone for Delta Rescue. Maria Bellafronto for S.F SPCA, PETA, Fund for Animals, ASPCA and Humane Society of The United States. Ernest Der for Debra Dolch (Special Administrator and nominated Trustee Ad Litem).

Mr. Hood stated that he does not have new Counsel, explained reasons why he is having difficulty in obtaining new Counsel and objected to appointment of Debra Dolch as Trustee Ad Litem. Ms. Bellafronto and Mr. Hess presented oral argument to support appointment of Debra Dolch. The Court granted the Motions to Be Relieved As Counsel in both matters (subject to review of the orders). The Court also granted the Ex Parte Petition for Appointment of Debra Dolch as Trustee Ad Litem (subject to review of the order). The motions on calendar for June 6, 2014 were continued to July 9, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary and the Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. were continued to July 23, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition To Remove Trustee, For Damages, Equitable Relief To Invalidate Trust & Appoint Fiduciary
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Continued To May-27-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-14-297520 The Estate of Ellen A. Hood and PTR-14-297521 The Ellen A. Hood Revocable Trust

Appearances: Walter Shjeflo for and with George Hood. Joseph Corey for Edward Corey (Trustee). William Hess appeared by telephone for Delta Rescue. Maria Bellafronto appeared by telephone for S.F SPCA, PETA, Fund for Animals, ASPCA and Humane Society of The United States.

Mr. Hood stated that he can not move forward with Mr. Shjelfo, that they are no longer in communication, that it is hostile and that he did not agree to appoint Debra Dolch. Mr. Hood also stated that he is dealing with the foreclosure and raised the issue of breach of fiduciary duty with regard to the issue of the mortgage. The Court raised the issue of Mr. Hood having no authority to deal with the foreclosure, and stated that it is the mind set to appoint a Trustee Ad Litem to deal with the foreclosure and perhaps sell the property. Discussion was held with regard to what took place at the last hearing, Mr. Hood seeking new Counsel and whether a Motion to be Relieved as Counsel or a Substitution of Attorney would be filed. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary and the Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. was continued to May 27, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. All discovery and all motion practice will be stayed until May 27, 2014. The Court invites the Parties to file an ex parte application for appointment of Debra Dolch as Trustee Ad Litem.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Julieann Hamill, CSR# 5151, (650) 588-7404. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Jones. (Reported)

Petition To Remove Trustee, For Damages, Equitable Relief To Invalidate Trust & Appoint Fiduciary
Off Calendar Apr-29-2014 Continued To May-13-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-14-297520 The Estate of Elen A. Hood and PTR-14-297521 The Ellen A. Hood Revocable Trust

Appearances: Walter Shjeflo for and with George Hood. Joseph Corey for Edward Corey (Trustee). Maria Bellafronto for PETA, S.F. SPCA, Fund for Animals, ASPCA and Humane Society of The United States. William Hess appeared by telephone for Delta Rescue. Joyce Driscoll and Sean Driscoll also present.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the appointment of George Hood, Mr. Corey being the Trustee, the house going into foreclosure, appointment of a Special Administrator to mashal assets and sell the house, questions not being answered and documents not being provided by Mr. Corey, the Parties not waiving objections to the 1985 Will, etc. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary and the Petition to Remove Trustee, etc. were continued to May 13, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 to see if a Trustee Ad Litem and a Special Administrator is in place, and for a referral to Department 206 for trial.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
